description: PRECONDITION (already in working tree as licval WIP, verification-only — do NOT rebuild): admin password is set at install. server/scripts/create-tenant.ts reads INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD (and args.password) and threads it through createTenantComplete → createAdminUser, which uses input.password ?? generateSecurePassword() (ee/server/src/lib/testing/tenant-creation.ts). create-tenant.ts also initializes tenant_settings (onboarding pending) and honors DB_HOST/DB_PORT/DB_USER_ADMIN. INITIAL_TENANT_ID lands in these same two files.
Migration: alter claim_codes.entitlement_id to NULLABLE (alga-license/migrations/03 baseline is NOT NULL). New numbered migration, up + down.
Migration: add claim_codes.tenant_id uuid NOT NULL with FK → tenant_registry(tenant_id), plus an index on tenant_id.
db.ts: insertClaimCode accepts tenant_id (required) and entitlement_id (optional/nullable).
db.ts: getClaimCode + ClaimCodeRow expose tenant_id and nullable entitlement_id.
db.ts: new revokeClaimCodesForTenant(tenantId) — reissue path for essentials codes that have no entitlement to key on.
db.ts: setRegistryTenantInstalled(tenantId) — set status='installed' + installed_at.
/register: source tenant_id from the claim_code row (row.tenant_id), the registry-minted identity, instead of the request body.
/register: keep accepting body tenant_id ONLY as a legacy fallback for pre-registry appliances (registry path ignores it).
/register: essentials path — when row.entitlement_id is null, skip license minting and return no token, but still upsertAppliance (credential + tenant_id).
/register: look up tenant_registry by tenant_id for edition + company_name + contact_email to include in the response.
/register: paid path unchanged — mint per-appliance license bound aud=tenant_id (registry-sourced), reuse license_sub, return first_jwt + appliance_credential + check_in_url.
RegisterResponse type + payload: add tenant_id, edition, company_name, contact_email; make appliance_credential / first_jwt / check_in_url optional (present only for paid).
/register: on success, set registry status='installed' + installed_at (F007).
/register-tenant: new route scaffold guarded by makeServiceAuthHook (service auth), registered in server.ts.
/register-tenant: create tenant_registry row via createRegistryTenant (deployment_type=appliance, status=registered) from company/contact/edition; validate edition enum.
/register-tenant: paid tier — create/link the entitlement (+ stripe_customer_id linkage) for the new tenant.
/register-tenant: mint an install code carrying tenant_id (+ entitlement_id when paid) via generateClaimCode + insertClaimCode (F004).
/register-tenant: presign the current ISO and return { tenant_id, install_code, download_url } (RegisterTenant request/response api-types).
/install-codes/reissue: new route scaffold guarded by makeServiceAuthHook.
/install-codes/reissue: resolve the registry tenant by contact_email (getRegistryTenantByEmail) or tenant_id (getRegistryTenant); 404 when unknown.
/install-codes/reissue: revoke prior unconsumed codes for that tenant via revokeClaimCodesForTenant (F006) so only the newest code is live.
/install-codes/reissue: mint a fresh install code for the SAME tenant_id (+ current entitlement if any) and a fresh presigned download_url; return { install_code, download_url }.
Presign helper in alga-license: holds MinIO credentials (config/env), mints a time-boxed presigned GET URL for an object key. (Decision pending §16.1.)
Appliance release process publishes the current generic ISO to a known object-store key that the presign helper targets.
Presigned URL expiry is configurable (claim-code-aligned TTL) and documented.
createTenant (tenant-creation.ts) accepts optional tenantId; when set, tenantInsert.tenant = input.tenantId instead of relying on the gen_random_uuid() default.
Thread tenantId through createTenantComplete (TenantCreationInput) down to createTenant.
server/scripts/create-tenant.ts reads INITIAL_TENANT_ID (env + optional arg) and passes it into createTenantComplete.
Appliance bootstrap (configmap/script that runs create-tenant at first boot) sets INITIAL_TENANT_ID from the redeem result.
INITIAL_TENANT_ID unset → unchanged DB-generated UUID behavior (additive/safe, no regression for non-registry installs).
Idempotent install: rerunning create-tenant with the same INITIAL_TENANT_ID no-ops rather than duplicating the tenant/admin (admin-user create already guards on (email, tenant)).
Setup UI: add an 'enter install code' step (alongside admin password) in the appliance first-boot setup flow.
Host-service redeem: POST { claim_code, appliance_id } to /register from the install-time consumer (generalize connectAppliance).
Host-service redeem: parse tenant_id + edition (+ optional license bits) from the richer RegisterResponse (F015).
Install: run create-tenant with INITIAL_TENANT_ID = redeemed tenant_id (wires F052/F053 to the redeem result).
Seed license_state with edition for every install (essentials included), so the appliance reads its tier from license_state.
Seed license_state with license_token (first_jwt) + appliance_credential + check_in_url for paid installs (existing connectAppliance write, made token-optional).
Essentials install: license_state row exists with no token; appliance runs at edition=essentials without erroring on the missing license.
Setup UI surfaces invalid/expired/consumed code errors clearly and points the user to portal re-issue.
Setup UI/host-service blocks install with a clear 'license service unreachable' error when alga-license can't be reached at redeem.
nm-store: registration form collects company, contact name/email, edition (and product_code) for an appliance registration.
nm-store: registration submit calls alga-license POST /register-tenant (service-authed) and handles the response.
nm-store: confirmation page shows the install code + download link.
nm-store: email the install code + download link to the contact.
nm-store: paid tier — Stripe checkout ordering (registry row at submit; entitlement + code mint on checkout.session.completed). (Decision pending §16.2.)
nm-store: portal 're-issue install code' action (behind portal auth) calls POST /install-codes/reissue and shows the fresh code + link.
Docs: update appliance install + licensing docs to describe register → download → install → re-issue (operator-facing).
